step.title: Step 35
step.actionDisplay: UP -> UP -> UP -> UP -> UP -> UP -> UP -> UP -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> DOWN -> DOWN -> DOWN

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! The blocks moved differently this time:
- After 8 UP: cols 29-33, rows 35-36 (not cols 34-38, rows 5-6 as before)
- After 3 RIGHT: cols 34-38, rows 35-36
- After 3 DOWN: cols 34-38, rows 40-41
So the blocks didn't move to the top of the maze this time. They only moved 5 rows up (from rows 40-41 to rows 35-36). This is different from before, when 8 UP moved the blocks from rows 40-41 to rows 5-6 (35 rows up).
Wait, but the blocks were at cols 29-33, rows 40-41 at the start (after the game reset). After 8 UP, they moved to cols 29-33, rows 35-36. That's 5 rows up, not 35 rows.
But earlier, when the blocks were at cols 34-38, rows 40-41, 8 UP moved them to cols 34-38, rows 5-6. That's 35 rows up.
So the movement depends on the column position. When the blocks are at cols 29-33, UP moves them 5 rows. When the blocks are at cols 34-38, UP moves them 35 rows.
This suggests that the maze has different passage widths or structures at different column positions. The blocks at cols 29-33 are in a narrower passage, while the blocks at cols 34-38 are in a wider passage.
Let me re-examine the maze structure. Looking at the ascii output:
- Row 40: ccGBBBBBBBGccccccccGGGGGOOOOOcccccGGGGGGGGGGG
- The middle passage is at cols 29-38 (GGGGGOOOOO = 10 chars).
- The blocks at cols 29-33 are in the left part of the middle passage.
- The blocks at cols 34-38 are in the right part of the middle passage.
When the blocks are at cols 29-33, UP moves them 5 rows. When the blocks are at cols 34-38, UP moves them 35 rows. This suggests that the passage at cols 29-33 is shorter than the passage at cols 34-38.
Actually, looking at the maze structure, the middle passage at rows 40-44 is at cols 29-38. The blocks at cols 29-33 are in the left part of the passage, and the blocks at cols 34-38 are in the right part of the passage.
When the blocks go UP from cols 29-33, they move 5 rows up to rows 35-36. This is the top of the middle passage.
When the blocks go UP from cols 34-38, they move 35 rows up to rows 5-6. This is the top of the right passage (through the top horizontal passage).
So the maze has a connection between the middle passage and the right passage at the top. The blocks at cols 34-38 can go UP through the top horizontal passage to the right passage.
Given this, the path to reach the white cross is:
1. Go RIGHT from cols 29-33 to cols 34-38 (1 RIGHT action).
2. Go UP from cols 34-38 to rows 5-6 (8 UP actions).
3. Go RIGHT from cols 34-38 to cols 49-53 (3 RIGHT actions).
4. Go DOWN from rows 5-6 to rows 20-21 (3 DOWN actions).
But the game ended after step 4 last time. Let me try a different approach.
Actually, looking at the current position, the blocks are at cols 34-38, rows 40-41. This is the same position as before the game ended. Let me try going UP from here to reach the top of the maze.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Blocks at cols 34-38, rows 40-41
# Try going UP to reach the top of the maze
result = action(['UP', 'UP', 'UP', 'UP', 'UP', 'UP', 'UP', 'UP'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'O':
        print(f"After 8 UP - Orange: {n['boundary']}")
        break
print("Game over:", result.get('game_over', False))
print("Level completed:", result.get('level_completed', False))
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL RESULT: python
sections.content:
After 8 UP - Orange: [[35, 29], [35, 33], [36, 33], [36, 29]]
Game over: False
Level completed: False
